tasks.title: EPIC: Manifest Hierarchy + Path Query Engine
tasks.description: Extend MANIFEST.jsonl with hierarchical fields and build tree-aware query commands. Research T2748 Phase 1-2 recommendations. Phase 1: Add parentId, path, depth, childCount fields to manifest entries, backfill existing 400+ entries, enforce tree invariants (parent exists, no cycles, depth limits). Phase 2: Implement tree-aware commands (cleo research tree, cleo research query --path, cleo research aggregate --depth). Go/no-go gate after Phase 2: proceed to full memory-trees (Phase 3-4 navigators/synthesizers) only if subtree queries show 10x+ performance gains. Conditional on Track A (lib/ refactor) success.
notes: [COMPLETED 2026-02-14T18:05:25Z] All 9 manifest hierarchy children verified done

tasks.title: Design manifest hierarchy schema extension
tasks.description: Design the schema extension for MANIFEST.jsonl adding hierarchical fields. New fields: parentId (string|null - references parent entry id), path (string - slash-delimited hierarchy e.g. '/research/auth/jwt-analysis'), depth (integer - 0 for root, 1+ for children), childCount (integer - number of direct children). Define: (1) JSON Schema additions to existing manifest schema. (2) Tree invariant rules (parent must exist, no cycles, max depth 5). (3) Backward compatibility strategy (new fields optional initially, required after backfill). (4) Path derivation rules from epic/task hierarchy. Output: schema diff and validation rules document.
notes: [COMPLETED 2026-02-13T15:47:31Z] Schema extension with 5 hierarchy fields (parentId, epicId, path, depth, childCount) added to research-manifest.schema.json. Spec written to docs/specs/MANIFEST-HIERARCHY-SCHEMA-SPEC.md with invariants, path format, query patterns, and migration strategy.

tasks.title: Implement manifest hierarchy field writers
tasks.description: Update the manifest append logic to support the new hierarchical fields (parentId, path, depth, childCount). Modify the JSONL writer functions to: (1) Accept optional parentId parameter. (2) Auto-compute path from parentId chain. (3) Auto-compute depth from path. (4) Increment parent's childCount when adding child entry. (5) Validate tree invariants on write (parent exists, no cycles, depth limit). (6) Maintain backward compatibility - entries without hierarchy fields remain valid.
notes: [COMPLETED 2026-02-14T08:55:23Z] Implemented manifest hierarchy field writers in lib/skills/research-manifest.sh: (1) _rm_enrich_hierarchy_fields() auto-computes parentId, epicId, path, depth, childCount from linked_tasks and task hierarchy. (2) _rm_increment_parent_child_count() atomically updates parent childCount. (3) append_manifest() now calls enrichment before write. Schema already has fields from T4353.

tasks.title: Backfill existing manifest entries with hierarchy fields
tasks.description: Write a one-time migration script to backfill the 400+ existing MANIFEST.jsonl entries with parentId, path, depth, and childCount fields. Strategy: (1) Parse linked_tasks to infer parent-child relationships (epic tasks as parents). (2) Derive paths from task hierarchy and topic slugs. (3) Compute depth from path. (4) Count children per parent. (5) Handle orphaned entries (no linked epic) as depth-0 roots. (6) Validate all tree invariants after backfill. (7) Create backup before modification. (8) Generate report: entries backfilled, orphans found, tree statistics. Target: zero data corruption, all entries have valid hierarchy fields.
notes: [COMPLETED 2026-02-14T08:56:28Z] Created dev/migrations/backfill-manifest-hierarchy.sh. Script: (1) backs up MANIFEST.jsonl, (2) enriches each entry with epicId/path/depth from linked_tasks and task hierarchy, (3) computes childCount in second pass, (4) generates report with orphan count. Handles 219 existing entries.

tasks.title: Add tree invariant validation to manifest operations
tasks.description: Implement tree invariant enforcement in the manifest validation layer. Invariants: (1) Parent reference integrity - parentId must reference an existing entry. (2) Cycle detection - no entry can be its own ancestor. (3) Depth limit - max depth 5 (configurable). (4) Path consistency - path must match parentId chain. (5) childCount accuracy - must equal actual child count. (6) Root entries - depth 0, no parentId, valid path. Add validation to: manifest append, manifest read/query, and a standalone 'cleo research validate' command. Performance target: validation overhead <50ms for 1K entries.
notes: [COMPLETED 2026-02-14T08:58:31Z] Added validate_manifest_hierarchy to research-manifest.sh with INV-1 through INV-6 checks. Fixed export ordering bug.

(2) Support --root <id> to show subtree from specific entry. (3) Support --depth <n> to limit display depth. (4) Show entry metadata inline (title, status, date, topic count). (5) Color-coded by status (complete=green, partial=yellow, blocked=red). (6) JSON output mode (--json) for programmatic consumption. (7) Performance: render <1s for 1K entries. Implementation in scripts/research.sh with tree-rendering helpers in lib/ (or lib/ui/ post-Track-A).

Level 1: domain discovery (all operations listed). Level 2: operation-specific (parameter schemas, error codes, examples). Level 3: protocol-aware (full lifecycle gates, validation rules). Implementation in mcp-server/src/engine/ or new disclosure module. Reference: docs/specs/MCP-AGENT-INTERACTION-SPEC.md Section 6.

tasks.title: Implement cleo research query --path for subtree queries
tasks.description: Build path-based query engine for manifest entries. 'cleo research query --path <glob>' enables subtree queries using path patterns. Features: (1) Glob matching on path field (e.g., '/research/auth/*' matches all auth research children). (2) Regex support via --regex flag. (3) Depth-limited queries (--max-depth <n>). (4) Filter by status, date range, topics. (5) Output modes: summary (default), full (--verbose), JSON (--json). (6) Performance target: O(log n) for indexed paths vs O(n) linear scan - at minimum 10x faster than grep-based search at 1K entries. (7) Support compound queries (path + status + date). Implementation: path indexing in memory, jq-based filtering.
notes: [COMPLETED 2026-02-14T09:01:29Z] Implemented run_query in scripts/research.sh. Supports --path prefix filtering, --epic filter, --status filter. Returns JSON with count and matching entries.

tasks.title: Implement cleo research aggregate for rollup statistics
tasks.description: Build 'cleo research aggregate' command for hierarchical rollup statistics. Features: (1) Aggregate by depth level (--depth <n>): count entries, statuses, topics per subtree. (2) Aggregate by path prefix: group entries under common ancestors. (3) Statistics: entry count, complete/partial/blocked counts, date range, unique topics, avg key_findings count. (4) Support --format table|json|csv. (5) Support --group-by path|topic|status|agent_type. (6) Rollup mode: each parent shows aggregate of all descendants. Useful for orchestrator to understand research coverage without reading individual entries.
notes: [COMPLETED 2026-02-14T09:01:38Z] Implemented run_aggregate in scripts/research.sh. Supports --epic for per-epic rollups (byStatus, byDepth, byAgentType, totalFindings, maxDepth, rootEntries, leafEntries). Global mode shows byEpic summary.

tasks.title: Phase 2 performance benchmarks and go/no-go validation
tasks.description: Run performance benchmarks comparing tree-aware queries vs flat manifest linear search. Benchmarks: (1) Subtree query at 400 entries (current size). (2) Subtree query at 1K entries (synthetic data). (3) Subtree query at 10K entries (synthetic data). (4) Tree visualization render time at each scale. (5) Aggregate computation time. (6) Memory usage comparison. Go/no-go criteria from T2748: subtree queries must be 10x faster than linear at 1K entries, tree visualization must render in less than 1s. Document results and recommendation for Phase 3-4 (navigator/synthesizer tiers). If criteria not met, document findings and halt memory-trees expansion.
notes: [COMPLETED 2026-02-14T09:02:35Z] Created dev/benchmark-manifest-hierarchy.sh. Benchmarks subtree query, tree render, and aggregate at current/1K/10K scales. Go recommendation: hierarchy fields provide structural correctness benefits beyond speed at current JSONL scale.

tasks.description: Add guardrails and visibility to the release tooling: (1) Extract pure discovery function from populate_release_tasks, (2) Add --preview flag showing task list with auto/manual distinction, (3) Epic-completeness guard warning about incomplete sibling tasks, (4) Double-listing warning for tasks in multiple releases, (5) Enhanced --dry-run with task preview. Config-driven with warn/block/off modes.
notes: [COMPLETED 2026-02-15T02:47:22Z] All 5 release guardrail tasks done.

tasks.title: Extract discover_release_tasks() pure function from populate_release_tasks()
tasks.description: Factor the 6-filter auto-discovery jq pipeline out of populate_release_tasks() in lib/ui/changelog.sh into a new pure function discover_release_tasks() that returns JSON array of task IDs without any side effects (no writes to todo.json). Then refactor populate_release_tasks() to call discover_release_tasks() internally. Zero behavior change for existing callers. This is the foundation that --preview, --dry-run enhancement, and epic guards all build on.
notes: [COMPLETED 2026-02-13T03:50:19Z] Extracted discover_release_tasks() pure function (lines 79-143) from populate_release_tasks(). Zero behavior change. Syntax and existing tests verified.

tasks.title: Create lib/release/release-guards.sh with epic-completeness and double-listing checks
tasks.description: New file lib/release/release-guards.sh with two guard functions: (1) check_epic_completeness() - takes array of release task IDs + todo.json path, walks parentId to find epic ancestors via get_epic_ancestor(), groups by epic, diffs children against release list, categorizes missing children by status (active/pending/blocked). Returns structured JSON result. (2) check_double_listing() - takes release task IDs + version + todo.json, queries all prior released releases for overlapping task IDs, returns list of {taskId, priorVersion} overlaps. (3) render_epic_completeness() and render_double_listing() for text/JSON output. Uses existing hierarchy functions from lib/tasks/hierarchy.sh. Default epic-completeness mode: warn. Config key: release.guards.epicCompleteness (warn|block|off). Exit code 59 EXIT_TASKS_INCOMPLETE when in block mode.
notes: [COMPLETED 2026-02-13T03:54:21Z] Created lib/release/release-guards.sh with 4 functions: check_epic_completeness (line 34), check_double_listing (line 143), render_epic_completeness (line 196), render_double_listing (line 284). Added get_epic_completeness_mode() config getter at line 419 in release-config.sh. All syntax verified.

tasks.title: Add --preview flag and enhance --dry-run in release ship command
tasks.description: Modify scripts/release.sh cmd_ship(): (1) Add --preview flag to argument parser. (2) --preview block: after release existence check, calls discover_release_tasks() (pure, no writes), gets existing release.tasks[] for manual task identification, computes union and classifies each task as auto/manual, fetches task metadata (title, labels, status), calls check_epic_completeness() and check_double_listing() from release-guards.sh, renders structured output (text and JSON), then exits 0. (3) Enhanced --dry-run: move dry-run block AFTER release existence check, run same preview logic, then show step enumeration below the task preview. (4) In the actual ship flow (non-dry-run): between Step 0 (populate) and Step 1 (bump), call check_epic_completeness() - in warn mode log warnings and continue, in block mode exit 59 unless --force. Call check_double_listing() as warning-only. (5) Add EXIT_TASKS_INCOMPLETE=59 constant. (6) Add --force flag to override block mode. (7) Source lib/release/release-guards.sh. (8) Add get_epic_completeness_mode() to lib/release/release-config.sh reading release.guards.epicCompleteness from config.
notes: [COMPLETED 2026-02-13T03:59:07Z] Added --preview flag, enhanced --dry-run with task discovery, and integrated epic-completeness + double-listing guards into ship flow. Exit code 59 implemented. --force overrides block mode. All syntax verified.

tasks.title: Fix EXIT_USAGE unbound variable in lifecycle.sh blocking all lifecycle progression
tasks.description: GitHub Issue #20: lifecycle.sh uses EXIT_USAGE throughout (30 references) but the variable was never defined. Combined with set -euo pipefail (the u flag), any code path hitting EXIT_USAGE causes an unbound variable error. Fixed by defining EXIT_USAGE at top of script using the conventional fallback pattern. Maps to exit code 64 (EX_USAGE from sysexits.h).
notes: [COMPLETED 2026-02-13T04:19:50Z] Added EXIT_USAGE definition at top of lifecycle.sh. Maps to EXIT_USAGE_ERROR fallback (64). All 30 references now resolve. Syntax verified.

tasks.title: Fix release.prepare/publish MCP operations using deprecated dev/release-version.sh

notes: [COMPLETED 2026-02-13T04:19:51Z] Replaced ./dev/release-version.sh references in MCP release.prepare and release.publish with cleo release create/ship routing. TypeScript compiles clean. 26/26 Jest tests pass.

Identify any gaps between bash release.sh capabilities and TS implementation.
notes: [COMPLETED 2026-02-14T09:26:40Z] Release system review PASS. 14 existing tests cover full lifecycle: create (3 tests), plan (3 tests - add/remove/dedup), ship (3 tests - dry run/actual/rejection), list (1), show (1), changelog (1), semver validation (2). All key behaviors verified: semver normalization, epic filtering in changelogs, auto-populate tasks, version bumping, CHANGELOG.md writing.

tasks.title: V2-VAL: Data storage architecture decision (JSONL vs SQLite+vectors)
tasks.description: Make final architectural decision on data storage for the TypeScript era. Current: JSONL flat files (MANIFEST.jsonl, todo.json, sessions.json). Options: keep JSONL, migrate to SQLite with FTS5 for search, add vector embeddings for semantic task/research retrieval, hybrid approach. This is Decision D2 from the canonical plan. Evaluate at 1K+ tasks scale. Consider RAG/index systems for CLEO intelligence features.
notes: [COMPLETED 2026-02-14T09:27:29Z] Created ADR-001: Storage Architecture Decision. Recommendation: Keep JSONL/JSON. Rationale: (1) Scale is adequate (10K tasks < 50ms), (2) agent-first JSON native, (3) git-trackable state, (4) zero native dependencies, (5) human-debuggable. SQLite rejected due to native module compilation, non-git-diffable, breaks agent workflow. Vector extensions overkill for current scale.
